runs.title: Morning Digest: Computational Materials (Top 10)
runs.summary: Ten newly published papers selected for direct relevance to MLIP development, atomistic simulation, DFT-guided screening, adsorption modeling, defect physics, and simulation-informed materials design.

articles.displayTitle: Density-fluctuation analysis sharpens atomistic picture of metallic-glass shear bands
articles.title: Computational study of density fluctuation-facilitated shear band formation in bulk metallic glasses
articles.summary: Uses atomistic modeling to resolve how local density fluctuations seed and steer shear-band formation in bulk metallic glasses. The work is useful for researchers building deformation workflows around local structural metrics, non-affine motion, and heterogeneity-sensitive constitutive analysis.
conclusions: The simulations connect density heterogeneity to the onset and propagation of localized shear rather than treating shear bands as purely emergent post-yield features.
The reported structure-response linkage is a practical cue for future MD studies of metallic-glass rejuvenation, annealing history, and mechanically driven disorder.

articles.displayTitle: Li-decorated C-doped BN graphyne remains a viable AIMD-screened storage motif
articles.title: DFT-D3 and ab initio molecular dynamics investigation of hydrogen storage in Li-decorated carbon-doped BN analogue of graphyne
articles.summary: Combines dispersion-corrected DFT with AIMD to test adsorption energetics, stability, and storage performance in a low-dimensional BN-graphyne analogue decorated with Li. It is relevant as a compact workflow example for coupling adsorption-site analysis with finite-temperature stability checks.
conclusions: Li decoration and carbon substitution reshape the adsorption landscape enough to make the platform competitive as a model hydrogen-storage surface.
AIMD validation matters here because the promising static adsorption picture is only useful if the decorated sheet remains dynamically stable under operating conditions.

articles.displayTitle: Doped MoS2 finally gets a purpose-built MLIP route for longer MD windows
articles.title: Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials Enable Molecular Dynamics Simulations of Doped MoS2
articles.journal: Journal of Chemical Theory and Computation
articles.summary: Develops machine-learned interatomic potentials for doped MoS2 so chemically perturbed 2D structures can be followed at MD scales that are inaccessible with routine DFT. This is directly relevant to LAMMPS or DeePMD users studying dopant effects on structural stability, diffusion, and thermally activated disorder.
conclusions: The study shows that dopant chemistry can be embedded in an MLIP without giving up the local bonding fidelity needed for defect-rich layered materials.
It provides a template for extending MLIP workflows from pristine monolayers to compositionally complex, device-relevant 2D systems.

articles.displayTitle: Heusler screening couples MLP energetics to transfer regressions for faster ranking
articles.title: Accurate screening of functional materials with machine-learning potential and transfer-learned regressions: Heusler alloy benchmark
articles.summary: Benchmarks a combined workflow in which machine-learning potentials generate scalable atomistic information and transfer-learned regressions convert that information into faster functional-material screening. Heusler alloys make this especially relevant for compositionally broad searches where DFT-only ranking is too expensive.
conclusions: The hybrid pipeline narrows the gap between force-model throughput and property-prediction fidelity for multi-component alloy discovery.
For high-throughput materials projects, it suggests a practical division of labor between potential training, atomistic sampling, and downstream regression.

articles.displayTitle: MLMD resolves SiC melting behavior that is hard to map with direct first-principles MD
articles.title: Incongruent melting and phase diagram of SiC from machine learning molecular dynamics
articles.summary: Applies machine-learning molecular dynamics to the high-temperature phase behavior of SiC and extracts melting characteristics and phase-boundary information at scales beyond routine ab initio MD. It matters for researchers studying extreme-condition ceramics, thermal stability, or phase coexistence with ML-accelerated sampling.
conclusions: The work uses MLMD to attack a thermodynamic regime where system size and simulation time are decisive, not just static energy accuracy.
Its treatment of incongruent melting is a good reference for phase-diagram workflows that need both fidelity and long-time sampling.

articles.displayTitle: Spatially mixed MLIPs target realistic multiregion simulations instead of monolithic fits
articles.title: Efficient and accurate spatial mixing of machine learned interatomic potentials for materials science
articles.summary: Introduces a scheme for spatially mixing machine-learned interatomic potentials, which is useful when different local environments or subsystems are best described by different models. That makes the paper technically relevant to heterogeneous interfaces, adaptive-resolution workflows, and practical LAMMPS integration strategies.
conclusions: Spatial mixing provides a route around the brittle all-in-one potential strategy for systems with sharply different bonding environments.
The method could reduce retraining burden in interface and defect problems where one universal model is unnecessarily hard to build.

articles.displayTitle: Tungsten embrittlement by hydrogen bubbles is now tractable at realistic atom counts
articles.title: Atomistic understanding of hydrogen bubble-induced embrittlement in tungsten enabled by machine learning molecular dynamics
articles.summary: Uses machine-learning molecular dynamics to study hydrogen bubble formation and embrittlement pathways in tungsten, a core plasma-facing material. The paper is valuable for defect and interface researchers because it connects large-scale atomistic sampling to a fusion-materials failure mechanism that is difficult to access with direct DFT alone.
conclusions: The MLMD treatment exposes how bubble evolution and local stress concentration couple to embrittlement at scales relevant to real microstructural damage.
It is a strong reference case for applying ML potentials to radiation, implantation, and gas-defect interactions in refractory metals.

articles.displayTitle: Automated self-optimizing MLP workflow pushes complex-material design closer to closed loop
articles.title: Self-optimizing machine learning potential assisted automated workflow for complex materials design
articles.summary: Presents a closed-loop style workflow where machine-learning potentials participate in an automated, self-optimizing search for complex materials. The paper is methodologically important for DP-GEN-style practitioners because it speaks directly to active data generation, autonomous refinement, and higher-throughput design loops.
conclusions: The workflow reduces manual intervention in potential improvement and candidate exploration, which is exactly the bottleneck in many modern MLIP projects.
Its value is less about one material system and more about a reusable pattern for automated model-and-structure co-optimization.

articles.displayTitle: 12-6-4 LJ parameterization improves adsorption modeling at open-metal MOF sites
articles.title: A Transferable Force Field for Simulating Adsorption in MOFs with Open Metal Sites Based on 12-6-4 Lennard-Jones Potential
articles.journal: Journal of Chemical Information and Modeling
articles.summary: Builds a transferable force field for adsorption in metal-organic frameworks with open metal sites using a 12-6-4 Lennard-Jones form that better captures ion-induced polarization effects. This is highly relevant for GCMC and MD workflows where generic force fields underperform for coordinatively unsaturated centers.
conclusions: The parameterization targets one of the most persistent failure modes in porous-material simulations: inaccurate adsorbate interactions near exposed metal centers.
If the transferability claims hold across families, the model can reduce the need for case-by-case refits in adsorption screening campaigns.

articles.displayTitle: DFT-validated ML ranking gives MOF photocatalyst screening a cleaner handoff
articles.title: Accelerating discovery through integration: a DFT validated machine learning framework for screening MOF photocatalysts
articles.summary: Integrates DFT validation with machine-learning screening for MOF photocatalysts, making it a good case study in how porous-material discovery can move from electronic-structure calculations to larger candidate libraries without discarding physical checks. It is useful for researchers working on adsorption-active or photoactive frameworks where structure-property mapping must stay tied to atomistic descriptors.
conclusions: The paper shows a practical handoff between first-principles validation and ML acceleration rather than treating the two as separate discovery tracks.
For porous energy materials, the framework is a workable template for narrowing large chemical spaces before committing to heavier DFT campaigns.

runs.title: Live Digest: Computational Materials (Top 10)
runs.summary: Fresh pull of ten recent materials papers with strong computational content across MD, DFT, machine-learned potentials, catalysis, battery materials, and simulation-informed experiments.

heroImage.alt: Graphical abstract for machine learning potential based ionic conductivity prediction in solid-state electrolytes.

articles.displayTitle: ML potentials now predict solid-electrolyte conductivity at screening scale
articles.title: Accelerating solid-state battery design: predicting ionic conductivity with machine learning potentials
articles.summary: Builds an MLP for the Li-P-S-Ge-Sn space and validates against DFT energies and forces; large-scale MD predicts room-temperature ionic conductivity with strong experimental correlation (R2 reported as 0.93), useful for rapid SSE screening workflows.
conclusions: A single MLP can reproduce DFT-level energetics for broad sulfide chemistry while remaining stable for long MD trajectories.
Predicted conductivity trends are sufficiently aligned with measurements to use as an early-stage candidate filter before expensive full workflows.

articles.displayTitle: MOF topology dominates hydrogen-storage ranking in large virtual libraries
articles.title: Computational investigation of the impact of metal-organic framework topology on hydrogen storage capacity
articles.summary: Screens 105230 topology-derived MOFs and combines GCMC data with ML ranking to identify high-performing nets for volumetric and gravimetric storage trade-offs, directly relevant to high-throughput adsorption screening pipelines.
conclusions: Network topology itself explains a major part of adsorption-performance variance, even before detailed chemistry tuning.
Hybrid GCMC plus ML workflows can reduce search space aggressively while retaining high-performing candidates.

articles.displayTitle: ML plus GA pipeline identifies lightweight BCC hydride alloys
articles.title: Design of lightweight BCC multi-principal element alloys with enhanced hydrogen storage using a machine learning-driven genetic algorithm
articles.summary: Integrates ML surrogate models, GA search, DFT analysis, and temperature-dependent MD to discover BCC MPEA hydrides with improved gravimetric capacity, a practical template for multi-objective composition optimization.
conclusions: Coupling surrogate inference with physics-based validation improves discovery efficiency for multicomponent alloy spaces.
Selected BCC compositions balance storage gain with acceptable thermodynamic and temperature-dependent behavior.

articles.displayTitle: MLP transport modeling resolves thermal anisotropy in selenium polymorphs
articles.title: Thermal conductivity of selenium crystals based on machine learning potentials
articles.summary: Uses ab initio-trained MLPs to study thermal transport in trigonal versus monoclinic selenium, linking structure-specific phonon behavior to transport and thermoelectric implications.
conclusions: Crystal phase and bonding anisotropy produce distinct thermal-conductivity signatures that can be captured with trained MLP dynamics.
MLP-based transport studies are practical for phase-comparative screening where direct ab initio dynamics are too costly.

articles.displayTitle: Dual-atom catalyst candidates for NRR screened via DFT-to-ML transfer
articles.title: Computational screening of M2N4-C-type dual-atom-catalysts for electrochemical ammonia synthesis by the first-principles DFT and machine learning
articles.summary: Evaluates DAC families with DFT and trains a transferable descriptor for NRR activity and pathway preference, demonstrating an efficient active-learning style route for catalyst down-selection.
conclusions: Descriptor-guided ML ranking reproduces DFT trends for key reaction intermediates across DAC motifs.
A reduced candidate shortlist can preserve promising NRR pathways while cutting expensive first-principles workload.

articles.displayTitle: DFT pathway map clarifies beta-Ga2O3 ALD growth chemistry
articles.title: DFT mechanistic insights into the atomic layer deposition of beta-Ga2O3
articles.summary: Maps ALD reaction pathways on beta-Ga2O3 using DFT energetics and transition states, giving process-level mechanistic guidance for precursor selection and growth-window tuning.
conclusions: Computed barrier hierarchy identifies the kinetically dominant reaction steps in the ALD cycle.
Precursor choice and pulse conditions can be tuned rationally from the mechanism instead of empirical trial-and-error.

articles.displayTitle: Cross-scale graphene modeling stack is now clearly consolidated
articles.title: Multiscale modeling of graphene and carbon nanostructures: advances in atomistic, coarse-grained, and machine learning approaches
articles.summary: A review that consolidates atomistic MD, coarse-grained, and ML-assisted frameworks for graphene-derived systems, useful as a method map for scale-bridging model development and force-field strategy.
conclusions: Recent workflows show that atomistic and coarse-grained layers can be connected without fully sacrificing key structure-property trends.
ML-assisted components are most impactful when used to accelerate bottleneck steps inside multiscale loops.

articles.displayTitle: Synthesis-plus-DFT workflow validates a lead-free double perovskite route
articles.title: Hydrothermal synthesis and comprehensive DFT evaluation of Ba2PrMnO6 double perovskite nanocrystals for next-generation spintronic and optoelectronic technologies
articles.summary: Combines synthesis with DFT-based electronic, optical, and mechanical analysis of a lead-free double perovskite; relevant as an experiment-computation coupling case for functional oxide screening.
conclusions: Experimental synthesis confirms that the targeted phase is accessible under practical processing conditions.
DFT descriptors align with measured behavior sufficiently to support computational pre-screening of related oxide chemistries.

articles.displayTitle: Janus monolayers show viable Ca-ion anode behavior in DFT/AIMD tests
articles.title: Unveiling the anodic potential of Janus MNS (M = Sc, Ti; N = Se, Te) monolayers for calcium-ion batteries: insights from DFT and AIMD studies
articles.summary: Uses DFT plus AIMD to quantify adsorption energetics, diffusion barriers, and voltage windows in Janus TMD anodes for Ca-ion storage, providing directly actionable descriptors for battery-anode screening.
conclusions: A subset of Janus monolayers satisfies simultaneous stability and migration criteria for Ca transport.
AIMD stability checks reinforce that descriptor-level rankings are not purely static-DFT artifacts.

articles.displayTitle: Alloyed AuPdCu nanoflowers gain EOR activity via electronic tuning
articles.title: Fine-tuning and electronic modulation of AuPdCu nanoflowers assembled with nanowires for robust ethanol oxidation reaction performance
articles.summary: Couples alloy design, catalytic testing, and DFT mechanistic analysis to tune intermediate binding and improve EOR activity, relevant for computationally guided catalyst composition optimization.
conclusions: Composition-controlled electronic restructuring shifts adsorption energetics in directions favorable for EOR kinetics.
DFT interpretation is consistent with observed catalytic improvements, supporting model-guided composition tuning.
